National Workshop · IIT Madras · April 2026
IIT Madras In-Person

Computational
Metagenomics

Methods & Applications

A three-day immersive workshop at IIT Madras designed for researchers, faculty, and students ready to decode complex microbial communities using state-of-the-art computational pipelines — led by RamanLab.

Register Now → View Schedule

IIT Madras

About the Workshop

Three Days of
Metagenomics

This national workshop covers the entire computational metagenomics pipeline — from raw sequencing data to biological insights. You'll work hands-on with real datasets and the exact tools used in published research.

Hosted by the Raman Lab & and delivered through NPTEL+, this is one of India's few intensive, in-person workshops on metagenomic data analysis.

  • Amplicon metagenomics & 16S rRNA analysis
  • Shotgun sequencing, assembly, and functional annotation
  • Statistical analysis, ML in microbiome research
  • Hands-on practicals every day with real datasets
  • Certificate of participation provided
📅
Dates
7th – 9th April, 2026
📍
Venue
NAC 2, IIT Madras, Chennai
🕘
Timings
09:00 AM – 06:00 PM IST
🎓
Mode
In-Person Only

🙏 A Warm Welcome Awaits You

We are delighted to welcome you to IIT Madras — one of India's finest research campuses, nestled inside a protected urban forest.

Whether you're joining us from across India or just across the city — expect brilliant science, wonderful peers, and a truly one-of-a-kind campus experience. Our team is here to make every moment count.

🌿 Protected Forest Campus 🦌 Blackbuck & Deer 🍽️ Campus Dining 🚌 Free Shuttle 🏥 On-Campus Hospital
Our Collaborators

Brought to You By
Four Pillars

This workshop is a joint effort across four institutions and programmes at IIT Madras — each bringing unique expertise, resources, and reach.

Research Group
Raman Lab
Prof. Karthik Raman's computational biology group at IIT Madras — leading the scientific programme of this workshop.
ramanlab.github.io ↗
Host School
WSAI, IIT Madras
Wadhwani School of Data Science & AI — providing institutional infrastructure and academic support for this workshop.
wsai.iitm.ac.in ↗
Research Centre
IBSE, IIT Madras
Centre for Integrative Biology and Systems Medicine — IIT Madras's hub for computational and systems biology research.
ibse.iitm.ac.in ↗
Platform Partner
NPTEL+
India's premier online learning platform — providing official registration, certification, and national outreach for the workshop.
elearn.nptel.ac.in ↗
Workshop Schedule

Programme
Overview

Click a day to expand the full session plan. Each day runs 9 AM – 6 PM with hands-on practicals throughout.

📄 Download Workshop Flyer
Day 01 Amplicon Metagenomics & 16S Analysis
Tue, 7 April 2026 ▼
09:00 – 10:00
Registration
Morning Session 1
10:00 – 10:15
Opening Remarks & Workshop Vision
10:15 – 11:00
Foundations of Metagenomics
What, why, and how of metagenomics
11:00 – 11:15☕ Tea Break
Morning Session 2
11:15 – 12:15
Introduction to 16S: Amplicon Theory
Principles, experimental design, workflows, and common challenges
12:15 – 13:00
Installation & Getting Started
CLI basics; hands-on tool setup

Afternoon Session 1

Data in Action (Part I)
Guided hands-on practicals with real datasets
16:00 – 16:15☕ Tea Break
Afternoon Session 2
16:15 – 17:00
Data in Action (Part II)
Workflow completion, troubleshooting, assignment
17:00 – 17:15
Buffer Zone & Wrap-up
Evening Lecture
17:15 – 17:45
Beyond the Bench: Applications & Future Perspectives
Clinical, environmental, and industrial perspectives
Day 02 Shotgun Sequencing, Assembly & Functional Annotation

Morning Session 1
09:00 – 09:45
Introduction to Shotgun Sequencing
Long read vs short read; Illumina, Nanopore, Oxford — choosing the right approach
09:45 – 11:00
Data Quality Checks & Pre-processing
FastQC · Trimmomatic · Kraken2 · Bracken
11:00 – 11:15☕ Tea Break
Morning Session 2
11:15 – 12:15
Assembly & Binning
Contig assembly · Binning · Bin refinement
12:15 – 13:45🍽️ Lunch Break
Afternoon Session 1
13:45 – 14:15
Quality Assessment & Taxonomic Classification
CheckM · GTDBTk
14:15 – 15:00
Functional Annotation
PROKKA · COGs
15:00 – 15:30
Assignment
15:30 – 15:45☕ Tea Break
Afternoon Session 2
15:45 – 16:15
Buffer Zone & Troubleshooting
16:15 – 16:45
Network Analysis
Evening Lecture
16:45 – 17:15
ML in Microbiome
Day 03 Statistical Analysis, ML & Capstone
Thu, 9 April 2026 ▼
Morning Session 1
09:00 – 10:00
Data Pre-processing for Statistical Analysis
10:00 – 10:15☕ Tea Break

Faculty & Experts

Learn from
the Experts

Led by researchers from the Wadhwani School of Data Science & AI, IIT Madras — at the frontier of computational biology in India.

Lead Instructor · Principal Investigator
Prof. Karthik Raman
Professor, Dept. of Data Science & AI · Wadhwani School of DS & AI (WSAI), IIT Madras

Prof. Karthik Raman leads the Raman Lab at IIT Madras, working at the crossroads of computational biology, systems biology, and machine learning. His research spans metabolic network modelling, microbiome data analysis, and ML-driven biological discovery — with numerous high-impact publications and open-source tools widely adopted by the research community.

🌐 Website 👥 Lab Members 🔗 LinkedIn 💻 GitHub
Instructor
Dr. Aarthi Ramachandran
Postdoctoral Researcher · Dept. of Data Science & AI, IIT Madras
LinkedIn Profile
Instructor
Dr. Pratyay Sengupta
Postdoctoral Researcher · Dept. of Data Science & AI, IIT Madras

Who Should Attend

Built for
Curious Minds

If you work with biology and want to apply computational power for microbial research, this workshop was designed for you.

🎓
UG Students (3rd Year+)
Background or interest in biology, keen to work with metagenomic data.
🔬
Researchers & PhD Students
Working with metagenomic or microbiome data, ready to level up your toolkit.
🧑‍🏫
Faculty Members
Looking to bring computational metagenomics methods into your research group.

On-Site Guide

Venue & Logistics

Everything you need to know before you arrive — location, transport, dining, and campus life.

Quick Guide
📍 Location
🗺️ How to Reach
🚌 Campus Bus
🍴 Dining
🏥 Hospital
⚠️ Requirements
🦌 Campus Life
📍Venue Location
VenueNAC 2 — New Academic Complex, Block 2
IIT Madras, Chennai 600036
RoomDA632 — 6th Floor
Nearest GateMain Gate (Sardar Patel Rd) · ~10 min walk
↗ View on Google Maps
🗺️How to Reach IIT Madras
✈️
By Air
Chennai International Airport — ~14 km away. Take a cab/auto to IIT Madras Main Gate on Sardar Patel Road.
🚆
By Train
Nearest stations: Chennai Central or Chennai Egmore. Take the MRTS to Kasturba Nagar/Taramani or Metro to Guindy, then an auto/cab to the campus.
🚌
By Bus
MTC buses (Routes 21G, 5C, 47G, 19B, 47) stop at IIT Madras Main Gate. Routes from Adyar, Guindy, and Taramani are convenient.
🚗
By Cab / Auto
Uber and Ola are widely available. Enter via Main Gate (Sardar Patel Road) — mention the Computational Metagenomics Workshop at NAC 2. Plan your travel accordingly to reach the campus at your convenience.
↗ Directions on Google Maps
🚌Getting Around Campus
🚗 Own Vehicle / Self-Drive

You can enter IIT Madras campus with your own vehicle via the Main Gate (Sardar Patel Road). Simply mention you're attending the Computational Metagenomics Workshop at NAC 2 (DA632).

🚦Campus Speed Limit: 20 km/h inside the campus at all times.
Gate to Use
Main Gate, Sardar Patel Rd
Parking Near Venue
NAC 2 Building
🚌 Campus Shuttle (Free Bus)

Free shuttle buses run frequently between the Main Gate and NAC. Frequency is approximately every 20 minutes.

🗺 Live Bus Tracker ⬇ Download Campus PDF Map
🍴Dining on Campus
1
Campus Canteen (CC)
Near the academic zone · Quick meals, snacks & beverages
2
CCD — Cafe Coffee Day
Inside campus · Coffee, sandwiches & light bites
3
Isthara Food Court
Multi-cuisine food court on campus · Order via app
Get Isthara App on Play Store
4
Zaitoon Restaurant
Inside campus · North Indian & Mughlai cuisine
5
Mess (Hostel Dining)
⚠️ Tokens required — purchase at CCW one day in advance for all 3 meals (breakfast, lunch, dinner)
🏠
Hostel Facility: For accommodation & hostel-related queries, visit the Centre for Cultural and Welfare (CCW) on campus.
🏥Medical Facility

IIT Madras has a full-service hospital on campus — available to all students, staff, and registered workshop visitors.

🏥
IITM Campus Hospital
On Campus · Open 24/7
Location Near NAC I, IITM
Services General OPD, emergency care, pharmacy
OPD Timings 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM · Emergency 24 hrs
For Visitors Workshop participants may access during the event
↗ Hospital on Google Maps
⚠️Mandatory Requirements

To receive your Certificate of Participation, the following are strictly enforced:

✓
100% Attendance
Present for all sessions — April 7–9, 2026 (09:00 AM – 06:00 PM IST)
✓
Daily Assignments
Completion of all hands-on practicals and assigned tasks each day
✓
Laptop with Setup
Bring a laptop with WSL, Conda and QIIME 2 pre-installed — see Installation guide
🦌Campus Life

IIT Madras is set inside a protected forest — state-of-the-art labs coexist with resident Blackbuck and spotted deer often spotted near the academic blocks. The campus also has a cricket ground, swimming pool, gymkhana, and the iconic Gajendra Circle.

🌿
Enjoy the Environment Responsibly
Please do not disturb the natural habitat and do not feed the animals — the deer and Blackbuck are wild and their diet and behaviour should not be altered by human contact.
